brief_justification: Arrives at exactly correct answer (10.37, No) with valid step-by-step simulation; identifies second-order overshoot-and-collapse dynamic and explains why predator success is self-undermining — genuine multi-level analysis; proceeds linearly through the table with no named assumptions, bias checks, or mid-analysis course corrections; table layout makes arithmetic checkable but no independent re-derivation or boundary-condition test is applied; uses '~' approximation and acknowledges fractional predators implicitly, but does not discuss model limitations (continuous vs. integer predators) or what would change the conclusion; mentions the biologist's wrong prediction but does not evaluate what conditions would make it correct or consider alternative rule interpretations; every intermediate value is shown in the table — prey growth, hunt subtraction, prey floor, predator adjustment — fully reproducible by a third party.
